title: United Airlines Orders 200 Additional 737 MAX Aircraft
summary: Massive fleet expansion signals sustained pilot hiring through 2030. United projects needing 10,000+ pilots over the next five years as it grows its mainline fleet and replaces retiring narrow-bodies.

title: Regional Airline First-Year Pay Now Exceeds $100K at Top Carriers
summary: Envoy, SkyWest, and Republic all offering $100K+ first-year compensation packages including bonuses and retention pay. A dramatic shift from pre-2023 rates when regional first officers earned under $50K. The pay increases are helping regionals compete for talent but attrition to mainline remains high.

title: FAA Proposes Updated R-ATP Hour Requirements for Military Pilots
summary: A new NPRM would adjust cross-country and instrument hour requirements for military R-ATP applicants, potentially reducing barriers for transitioning service members. The comment period is open through June 2026. Industry groups are largely supportive of the changes.

title: Delta Increases New-Hire Class Size to 45 Pilots Per Class
summary: Delta is expanding monthly class sizes amid accelerating retirements and network growth. Over 4,000 Delta pilots are eligible to retire in the next three years, and the airline is adjusting its hiring pipeline to maintain staffing levels as it adds routes to Europe and Asia.

title: DoD Expands SkillBridge Approved Flight Training Providers to 40+
summary: Fifteen new flight schools have been added to the SkillBridge approved provider list, including five Part 141 programs with direct airline pathway agreements. The expansion gives separating military members more options to use their final months of service for civilian flight training at no cost.

title: ATP-CTP Course Costs Stabilize Around $5,500 Industry-Wide
summary: After steady price increases in 2024-2025 driven by demand, ATP CTP course pricing has stabilized near $5,500. Several airlines including SkyWest and Envoy now cover the full ATP-CTP cost for pipeline candidates, removing a significant financial barrier for pilots approaching 1,500 hours.

title: Over 14,000 U.S. Airline Pilots Will Reach Mandatory Retirement Age by 2030
summary: A new analysis from the Regional Airline Association projects that more than 14,000 Part 121 pilots will hit the FAA's mandatory age-65 retirement limit within the next four years. The largest wave is expected at American, Delta, and UPS, where pilot seniority lists skew older. The data underscores sustained hiring demand across the industry.

title: Alaska Airlines Begins Pilot Integration with Hawaiian After Merger Close
summary: With the Alaska-Hawaiian merger now finalized, the combined airline is beginning pilot seniority list integration. Hawaiian new-hire pilot classes are paused during the transition, but Alaska mainline continues hiring at an accelerated pace to support the expanded Pacific network.

title: FAA Considers Expanding BasicMed to Include Additional Aircraft Categories
summary: The FAA is reviewing public comments on a proposed expansion of BasicMed privileges that could allow certificated pilots to fly larger single-engine aircraft without a traditional medical certificate. While not directly affecting Part 121 airline operations, the change could increase the pipeline of new private pilots who eventually pursue professional careers.

title: UPS Airlines to Hire 600+ Pilots in 2026 Amid Retirement Surge
summary: UPS Airlines announced plans to hire over 600 pilots this year as hundreds of senior captains reach the mandatory retirement age. The cargo carrier is offering some of the highest starting pay in the industry and has expanded its new-hire bases to include Miami and Dallas-Fort Worth.

title: Pilot Shortage Still Real But Moderating, Says Boeing Workforce Report
summary: Boeing's 2026 Pilot and Technician Outlook projects global demand for 612,000 new pilots through 2043, down slightly from prior estimates. In North America, the acute post-pandemic shortage has eased as training capacity expanded and regional pay increases improved retention. However, long-term demand remains strong due to retirements and global fleet growth.

title: NetJets Pilot Pay Now Competitive with Major Airlines Under New Contract
summary: A year after ratifying their new contract, NetJets pilots are seeing total compensation packages that rival legacy carriers. Senior captains on large-cabin aircraft report earnings above $400K annually. The improved pay is attracting experienced pilots who prefer the fractional schedule over traditional airline bidding and commuting.
